text: Code reviews are an essential part of the software development process. In a code review, a developer has their code checked by one or more colleagues before it is added to the main project. This practice helps catch bugs early, when they are much easier and cheaper to fix. More importantly, code reviews promote knowledge sharing within the team. By reading each other's work, developers learn new techniques and understand different parts of the codebase. This ultimately improves the overall quality of the team, as everyone becomes more skilled and the project becomes more reliable.
